summary: In 1976, Tracy Giefer Fullerman traveled to Germany to explore our family history. While there, she had a memorable experience when she got stranded at a bus stop in the middle of the night. A passerby, recognizing the name Giefer, connected her to a local Giefer relative who offered her shelter. This trip to Germany was the catalyst for the extensive family history project that eventually became our book.

Wilhelm Giefer was my great great great grandfather who built the house in Germany where Tracy stayed during her 1976 visit. He constructed that house himself in 1830, and it became a significant part of our family story. This connection helped spark the creation of our family encyclopedia, linking us directly to our German roots.

Ludwig Giefer was my great great great grandfather and the brother of Wilhelm Giefer, the man who built the house in Germany. In our family book, Ludwig’s story is given special attention with a full paragraph and pictures of him and his wife Gertrude. His lineage and children, including my great great grandfather JJ, are detailed carefully, showing how our family grew over generations.

Johannis Giefer is the common ancestor of all the Giefers documented in our family book. Born around 1720, his death certificate is one of the key documents that helped trace our family tree back ten generations to me, Sarah Elizabeth Giefer. His place in our history is foundational, connecting hundreds of relatives across time and geography.

The Minnesota Historical Society now holds a copy of our family history book, 'The Giefer Family in Germany and the United States.' Thanks to them, anyone interested can access this detailed record of our sprawling family tree. It’s wonderful to know that our family’s story is preserved and available to the public through such a respected institution.

entity: The Giefer Family in Germany and the United States
summary: 'The Giefer Family in Germany and the United States' is a two-volume, 700-page encyclopedia of our family history written by Tracy Giefer Fullerman. It includes genealogical records, stories, pictures, family trees, and maps showing the spread of Giefers in the US from the 19th to 20th centuries. This book is a treasure trove that connects me, born in 1993, to hundreds of ancestors and preserves our legacy for future generations.
